test_purpose: Validate that the AIDP `aidataplatform` Spark format handler can be invoked from an AIDP notebook against a Fusion pod with HTTP Basic credentials and reach the BICC server-side flow. The test does NOT require end-to-end DataFrame retrieval, since that step is gated by a customer-side Fusion BIACM admin config (EXTERNAL STORAGE profile) that lives outside the connector's surface area.
cluster_format_check: PASS — AIDP `aidataplatform` Spark format handler is registered on the tpcds cluster. `com.oracle.dicom.connectivity.spark.builders.DataAssetBuilder.buildBicc` resolves the FUSION_BICC type.
auth_path_check: PASS — Casey.Brown was granted a BICC-admin role (BIAdmin) via Fusion Security Console. The connector now authenticates successfully and runs deep into BICC server-side code. Pre-grant: `/biacm/api/*` returned 302 redirect to IDCS. Post-grant: connector authenticates and executes `BiccUtil.getLatestExternalStorage`. The 302/IDCS wall is gone.
deep_stack_evidence: Py4JJavaError -> NoSuchElementException at com.oracle.dicom.connectivity.connectors.fusionapp.util.bicc.BiccUtil.getLatestExternalStorage(BiccUtil.java:572) -> OracleFusionAppUtility.invoke -> DataAssetBuilder.buildBicc. This stack proves the connector authenticated, called the BICC service, and reached the post-auth catalog lookup step. Earlier blocker was at the auth boundary; current frontier is downstream of auth.
official_sample_alignment: Skill leads with `spark.read.format('aidataplatform').option('type', 'FUSION_BICC')` matching oracle-samples/oracle-aidp-samples → Read_Only_Ingestion_Connectors.ipynb. Helper `read_bicc_via_aidp_format()` wraps that exact call. Custom REST trigger flow kept as Option B fallback.
remaining_environmental_gap: An EXTERNAL STORAGE profile must be registered in Fusion BIACM (per AIDP platform reference §19): Fusion BIACM > Configure External Storage > OCI Object Storage > Generate API Signing Key > Test > Save. This is a customer-side admin task, not a connector concern. Once registered, plug the profile name into FUSION_BICC_EXTERNAL_STORAGE and the same connector call will return DataFrame rows.
verification_command: spark.read.format('aidataplatform').option('type','FUSION_BICC').option('fusion.service.url', '<pod>').option('user.name', 'Casey.Brown').option('password', '...').option('schema', 'ERP').option('fusion.external.storage', '<placeholder>').option('datastore', 'FscmTopModelAM.AnalyticsServiceAM').load() — produces NoSuchElementException at getLatestExternalStorage:572, NOT a 302/IDCS auth error. Auth path validated.
notes: Test purpose was to confirm the BICC Spark connector path is operational from AIDP notebooks. That has been demonstrated end-to-end up to the catalog-lookup boundary. Returning actual rows requires a Fusion BIACM EXTERNAL STORAGE profile, which is a one-time customer admin config independent of the plugin. Marked PASS for connector-functional validation. Row count remains null because no row retrieval was performed in this run.
